rationale: Ambiguous numeric date should follow caller 'first' preference; both agree.
Ambiguous numeric date should follow caller 'first' preference; both agree.
Ambiguous numeric date should follow caller 'first' preference; both agree.
`results = 3.8, 10` is typically not a date in strict extraction contexts.
Money should not parse as date in this sentence.
`French 75` should not infer year 2075.
`pre-qualification may` should not infer a month date.
RFC822-like timestamp should resolve with full time/offset context.
ISO date adjacent to token should still parse.
`9/15/27 144A` should still recover the date token.
Malformed separators should not parse.
Relative date phrase should be interpreted as relative datetime.
`in 2 weeks` should be relative, not a month/day fallback.
`20 days ago` should resolve relative to reference date.
Duration phrase is useful as duration object even if not legacy datetime.
Spanish relative keyword should resolve as relative date.
Spanish tomorrow keyword should resolve as relative date.
French `dans 2 jours` is best represented as relative or duration, not spurious absolute date.
French today keyword should resolve as relative date.
German today keyword should resolve as relative date.
German tomorrow keyword should resolve as relative date.
Portuguese today keyword should resolve as relative date.
Portuguese tomorrow keyword should resolve as relative date.
Italian today keyword should resolve as relative date.
Italian tomorrow keyword should resolve as relative date.
Day-first Romance month-name date should parse.
Canonical month-name date should parse.
Simple date range text should parse both endpoints.
ISO UTC timestamps should parse with second precision.
`40 minutes` should be duration signal; legacy 2040 date is misleading.
Strict mode should reject incomplete month/year phrase.
Strict complete numeric date should parse.
Strict ordinal day phrase should parse.
Year-only sentence can reasonably infer current month/day anchor if enabled.
OCR superscript noise should not force a legacy-style date hallucination.
